title: Refund policy
text: Customers may request a refund for any subscription charge within 30 days of the charge date. Refunds are processed to the original payment method and typically appear on the customer's statement within 5 to 10 business days. Annual plans are eligible for prorated refunds based on remaining unused months. Trial-period refunds are automatic and require no support intervention. For double-charge incidents, support staff should refund the duplicate charge immediately upon verification and file an incident in the billing log. The refund policy does not cover partial-month usage on monthly plans.

title: Payment method updates
text: To update the payment method on file, customers can navigate to Account Settings then Billing then Payment Methods. The interface supports credit and debit cards, ACH transfers for US-based customers on annual plans, and wire transfers for enterprise contracts above $10,000 annually. Changes to the payment method take effect on the next billing cycle. To change the card used for the next renewal specifically, customers should contact support at least 7 days before the renewal date.

title: Password reset flow
text: Password reset emails are sent from no-reply@example.com. The reset link expires after 24 hours from the time the email is sent. If a customer clicks an expired link, they are returned to the login page with an option to request a new link. Customers using SSO providers (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace) cannot reset their password through this flow; they must reset through their identity provider. Reset emails are throttled to one per 15 minutes per account to prevent spam.

title: Two-factor authentication
text: Two-factor authentication (2FA) is supported via TOTP authenticator apps (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password) and SMS to a verified phone number. Authenticator app codes are preferred over SMS for security reasons. If a customer cannot receive their 2FA code, support staff can verify identity through the account-recovery flow which requires answering security questions and confirming the last 4 digits of the payment method on file. 2FA is mandatory for all users on Enterprise plans.

title: Salesforce integration setup
text: The Salesforce integration syncs contacts, accounts, and opportunities every 15 minutes. The first sync after enabling the integration may take up to 4 hours for accounts with more than 100,000 records. Field-level mappings can be customized in Account Settings then Integrations then Salesforce then Field Mapping. The integration uses OAuth and respects Salesforce field-level permissions of the connected user. If the integration breaks, the most common cause is a revoked OAuth token; support should ask the customer to reconnect through the OAuth flow.

title: Encryption at rest and in transit
text: All customer data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. Documents stored in the platform are encrypted with per-customer encryption keys managed in AWS KMS. Customers on Enterprise plans may bring their own KMS keys (BYOK) for additional control. The platform is SOC 2 Type II certified, ISO 27001 certified, and HIPAA-compliant for accounts that have signed a Business Associate Agreement (BAA). Compliance documentation is available in the Trust Center accessible from Account Settings.

title: API rate limits
text: The REST API enforces rate limits on a per-API-key basis. The Free tier allows 100 requests per minute. The Standard tier allows 1,000 requests per minute. The Enterprise tier allows 10,000 requests per minute. Bulk endpoints (bulk_create, bulk_update, bulk_delete) count as a single request regardless of payload size, but the payload size is capped at 200 items per call. When rate limits are exceeded, the API returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header indicating how many seconds to wait before retrying.

title: API key rotation
text: API keys can be rotated through Account Settings then Developers then API Keys. When a new key is generated, the previous key remains active for 24 hours, providing a grace period to update integrations. After 24 hours, the previous key is automatically revoked and any integration still using it will receive HTTP 401 Unauthorized errors. Customers should plan API key rotation during low-traffic windows and update their integrations within the grace period to avoid service interruption.
